{{Short description|Historic Japanese Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | native_name =御塚・権現塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Historic Japanese Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | native_name =御塚・権現塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Short description|Historic Japanese Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | native_name =御塚・権現塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Short description|Historic Japanese Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | native_name =御塚・権現塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Short description|Historic Japanese Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft}} | name = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | native_name =御塚・権現塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.

[[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.

It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Historic Japanese Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | native_name =御塚・権現塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[Daizenji Station]] on the [[Nishitetsu]] [[Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14860 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/42db2e8a-61d7-40ed-8d6b-283fb070b839 Kurume Tourism] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kurume.fukuoka.jp/1080kankou/2015bunkazai/3050kurumeshishi/files/historywalk10.pdf Kurume city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun]][[ja:御塚・権現塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11488904]] Wikidata name: Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Historic Japanese Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | native_name =御塚・権現塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.279722|N|130.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[:en:Kurume Domain|Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Daizenji Station|Daizenji Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14860 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/42db2e8a-61d7-40ed-8d6b-283fb070b839 Kurume Tourism] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kurume.fukuoka.jp/1080kankou/2015bunkazai/3050kurumeshishi/files/historywalk10.pdf Kurume city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun]][[ja:御塚・権現塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11488904]] Wikidata name: Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Historic Japanese Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | native_name =御塚・権現塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.279722|N|130.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[:en:Kurume Domain|Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Daizenji Station|Daizenji Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14860 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/42db2e8a-61d7-40ed-8d6b-283fb070b839 Kurume Tourism] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kurume.fukuoka.jp/1080kankou/2015bunkazai/3050kurumeshishi/files/historywalk10.pdf Kurume city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[:en:Kurume Domain|Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Daizenji Station|Daizenji Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14860 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/42db2e8a-61d7-40ed-8d6b-283fb070b839 Kurume Tourism] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kurume.fukuoka.jp/1080kankou/2015bunkazai/3050kurumeshishi/files/historywalk10.pdf Kurume city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[:en:Kurume Domain|Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Daizenji Station|Daizenji Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]] west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of [[:en:Kurume Domain|Kurume Domain]] who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]], and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Daizenji Station|Daizenji Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Daizenji Station|en|Daizenji Station|WD=Q5209854|lt=Daizenji Station}} on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|WD=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River}} west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of {{ill|Kurume Domain|en|Kurume Domain|WD=Q426826|lt=Kurume Domain}} who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}}, and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Daizenji Station|en|Daizenji Station|WD=Q5209854|lt=Daizenji Station}} on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Historic Japanese Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | native_name =御塚・権現塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.279722|N|130.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|16|48|N|130|28|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|WD=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River}} west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of {{ill|Kurume Domain|en|Kurume Domain|WD=Q426826|lt=Kurume Domain}} who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}}, and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Daizenji Station|en|Daizenji Station|WD=Q5209854|lt=Daizenji Station}} on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}

A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of {{ill|Kurume Domain|en|Kurume Domain|WD=Q426826|lt=Kurume Domain|1=Kurume Domain}} who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}}, and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Daizenji Station|en|Daizenji Station|WD=Q5209854|lt=Daizenji Station|1=Daizenji Station}} on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun'''|御塚・権現塚古墳}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Miyamoto, Daizenji neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931, with the area under protection expanded in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139494|title=御塚・権現塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ontsuka・Gongenzuka Kofun are located close to a low plateau on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|WD=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}} west of downtown Kurume. These two Kofuns were the central tombs of a cluster of more than 40 ''[[kofun]]'' that once existed near Daizenji-cho. Fragments of cylindrical clay ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were found in both tumuli, indicating that both were built around the same time in the late 5th century to early 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are believed to be that of the local ruling clan, the "Minuma no Kimi", which is mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''.
; Ontsuka Kofun The southwestern Ontsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1970 revealed that the main axis of the mound is 76 meters long, the diameter of the posterior circular portion is 67 meters with a height of 10 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular part is 22.5 meters. It is estimated that it was built around the 5th century. A sketch by Yano Kazusada, a scholar of {{ill|Kurume Domain|en|Kurume Domain|WD=Q426826|lt=Kurume Domain|1=Kurume Domain}} who surveyed the area in 1848, shows that it was surrounded by a triple moat and had a total length about 130 meters.The three layers of outer moats and outer embankments around the outer periphery have been confirmed by modern {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}}, and the outer diameter of the outer embankment was confirmed to be 123 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>The inside of the tomb is unknown, but as the top of the tumulus has been largely destroyed, so it is thought that to have been robbed in antiquity.
;Gongenzuka Kofun The Gongenzuka Kofun is located immediately to the northeast of the Ontsuka Kofun. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus surrounded by a double moat, with an outer embankment of approximately 150 meters in diameter. The tumulus itself is 55 meters in diameter, with a height of nine meters. The outer moat is partially reclaimed and can be crossed, but the inner moat is intact. Although pieces of ''haniwa'' and pottery have been found on the inner embankment, there is a lack of materials to determine the construction date of the tomb itself, and as it has never been excavated, and its internal structure is unknown.
Currently, the site is maintained as a park. It is approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Daizenji Station|en|Daizenji Station|WD=Q5209854|lt=Daizenji Station|1=Daizenji Station}} on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
